bug_report.summary: Page Style submenu shows “No Style” selected (and page unstyled) before user chooses it
steps_to_reproduce: Press Alt to show the menu bar.
Open View → Page Style submenu (do not click any Page Style option).
expected_behaviors: The page remains styled (green background, white/large text).
In the Page Style submenu, “Basic Page Style” is selected/checked by default.
actual_behaviors: The page is already unstyled (white background, default text) before selecting “No Style”.
In the Page Style submenu, “No Style” appears selected/checked before the user clicks it.

false_positive_reasons: LLM hallucination — predicted an non-executed action as executed

bug_report.summary: View > Page Style submenu loses the built-in “Basic Page Style” option when a titled stylesheet is present
steps_to_reproduce: Load the provided data: URL that injects an alternate stylesheet with title “LateStyle”.
Open menu bar (Alt) > View > Page Style submenu.
expected_behaviors: Page Style submenu should always include built-in entries such as “Basic Page Style” and “No Style”.
If a titled stylesheet exists, it should be listed in addition to the built-in options (not replacing them).
actual_behaviors: “Basic Page Style” entry is missing; submenu shows only “No Style” and “LateStyle”.
